questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Which of the following cannot act as an oxidising agent?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The table below gives the results of three titrations carried out with 0.200 M HCl to determine the molarity of a given NaOH solution using phenolphthalein as indicator. NaOH was taken in the burette and HCl was taken in a conical flask for the titrations.
$\begin{aligned}& \text{Titration}\\ & \text{No}\end{aligned}$ $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{HCl}}(\mathrm{mL})$ $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{NaOH}}(\mathrm{mL})$ $\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{NaOH}\text{moldm}}{}^{-3}$
I 21.4 19.3 0.222
II 18.6 16.8 0.221
III 22.2 21.1 0.210
The actual molarity of the prepared NaOH solution was $0.220$ $\mathrm{moldm}^{-3}$. Which among the following could be the reason for the wrong value obtained in titration III?
options: Number of drops of phenolphthalein added to the titration flask was more in this titration
The concentration of HCl was wrongly used as 0.250 M for the calculation of $\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{NaOH}}$
A few drops of NaOH solution were spilled outside the titration flask during titration
A few drops of the neutralized solution from titration II were left behind in the flask

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Among the following, the reaction/s that can be classified as oxidation- reduction is /are
I. $\quad \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7{ }^{2-}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{CrO}_4{ }^{2-}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
II. $\quad \mathrm{SiCl}_4(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{s}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{MgCl}_2(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{Si}(\mathrm{s})$
III. $\quad 6 \mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{l})+12 \mathrm{KOH}(\mathrm{l}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{KClO}_3(\mathrm{~s})+10 \mathrm{KCl}+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$
IV. $\quad 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: In $\mathrm{YBa}_2 \mathrm{Cu}_3 \mathrm{O}_{7-\mathrm{x}}$, a superconducting oxide that got George Bednorz and Karl Muller the Nobel prize in 1986, Cu can exist in both +2 and +3 oxidation states and their proportion depends on the value of ' $x$ '. In $\mathrm{YBa}_2 \mathrm{Cu}_3 \mathrm{O}_{7-0.5}$.
options: 0.5 moles of Cu are in +3 oxidation state
$5 \%$ of Cu is in +3 oxidation state
All the Cu is in +3 oxidation state
All Cu is in +2 oxidation state

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
While doing titration, a student recorded a burette reading of 10.0 mL for the neutralization of $10.0 \mathrm{~mL} \mathrm{NaHC}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{aq})$ with $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq})$. In a separate experiment, 10.0 mL of this $\mathrm{NaHC}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{aq})$ solution could be completely oxidized by 10.0 mL of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ in an acidic medium.
What would be the molarity of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ used by this student?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which of the following pairs of compounds can be stable while retaining the identity of each compound in the pair over a period of time?
I. $\mathrm{FeCl}_3, \mathrm{SnCl}_2$
II. $\mathrm{HgCl}_2, \mathrm{SnCl}_2$
III. $\mathrm{FeCl}_2, \mathrm{SnCl}_2$
IV. $\mathrm{FeCl}_3, \mathrm{KI}$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
In the following reaction, the values of $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}$ and $\mathbf{c}$, respectively are
$\mathbf{a} \mathrm{F}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathbf{b} \mathrm{OH}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightarrow \mathbf{c} \mathrm{F}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathbf{d} \mathrm{OF}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathbf{e} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$

questions.q: Among (i) HCl , (ii) HOClO , (iii) $\mathrm{HOClO}_2$ and (iv) $\mathrm{HOClO}_3$, which cannot undergo a disproportionation reaction?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
$\mathrm{Br}_2$ disproportionates to $\mathrm{Br}^{-}$and $\mathrm{BrO}_3^{-}$in a hot alkaline solution as
$3 \mathrm{Br}_2+6 \mathrm{OH}^{-} \rightarrow 5 \mathrm{Br}^{-}+\mathrm{BrO}_3^{-}+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
The equivalent weight of $\mathrm{Br}_2$ is: $\left(\mathrm{M}=\right.$ molar mass of $\left.\mathrm{Br}_2\right)$
